text: Disk pressure runbook: when the WAL volume nears the configured cap, pause heavy consolidation and inspect compaction backlog before increasing retention.
Donor-module cleanup belongs in a separate maintenance branch; keep retry budget notes with that branch instead of service setup.
Release images build for linux amd64 and linux arm64 from the release gate; defer Docker Hub publishing until a later registry pass.
Embedder dimension belongs to store identity; changing model dimension requires a fresh volume or an explicit migration plan.
Doctor should emit structured JSON for WAL codec failures and keep recovery errors actionable even when server startup still works.
HTTP MCP accepts multiple bearer token bindings, one token per agent principal, so audit attribution follows the accepted token.
Plugin update output should show installed version and cache state so stale MCP manifests are visible during setup.
Volume migration uses a stopped-server snapshot of the data directory; preserve owner-only permissions and WAL files during restore.
Recall explanations should expose route, embedder state, active signal weights, and per-memory contribution ranks.
A local OCI runtime can run the published image for smoke tests; source builds stay on the standard Docker build path.
MCP token rotation should overlap old and new bearer token bindings, update clients, verify a read-only request, then remove the retired binding.
Public PR summaries should describe code and behavior changes only; omit private labels, local filesystem paths, personal context, and secret-shaped values.
docs/mcp-clients.md records that host-verified principal parameters must be explicit and the built-in MCP server must not infer identity from a connection.
Aionforge Memory is a Rust long-term memory layer for AI agents, with project identity, MCP client setup, principal authorization, recall, and audit documentation.
